scenario: Subprocess "Verify Documents" needs local scratch variable verificationResults scoped to subprocess only. Only clean boolean allDocumentsVerified propagates to parent.
question: Configuration?

options.text: Configure an Input Mapping on the Embedded Subprocess that explicitly copies all required parent-scope variables into the subprocess scope. This is necessary because the subprocess cannot read parent variables directly -- only variables explicitly mapped into the subprocess are accessible. The verificationResults scratch variable would stay local by default. Explicit input mappings are only needed to create subprocess-local copies or to rename variables -- they are not required for read access.

Replace the Embedded Subprocess with a Call Activity that references a separate child process definition. This provides strong scope isolation -- the child process has its own variable scope entirely separate from the parent, and only values passed via input/output mappings are shared. For a simple local scratch variable scenario within a single process, a Call Activity is over-engineered. The requirement is fulfilled more cleanly by an Embedded Subprocess with appropriate output mappings.

verified) and target allDocumentsVerified. When the subprocess completes, Zeebe applies the output mapping, writing the computed boolean to the parent scope. All other subprocess-local variables including verificationResults are automatically discarded when the subprocess ends without needing explicit cleanup.

Camunda 8 Embedded Subprocesses do not support subprocess-local variables -- any variable written inside the subprocess is immediately available in the parent scope. To create a logical separation, the team must use a naming convention such as prefixing all internal variables with verify_ to distinguish them from parent variables. Variables created inside a subprocess remain in the subprocess scope until explicitly promoted via output mappings -- they are not automatically propagated to the parent scope.

a.text: Incorrect. Embedded Subprocess has implicit read access; variables scoped to subprocess are local + discarded automatically. Output mapping promotes specific values to parent.

b.text: Incorrect. Implicit read access; explicit mapping redundant.

c.text: Correct. Call Activity = separate process instance; heavier than needed.

d.text: Incorrect. Subprocess-local variables ARE supported via scoped output mappings.

explanation: Call Activity = separate process instance; heavier than needed.
